questions.question: A music producer wants to mix two audio frequencies: $3sqrt{2}$ Hz and $2sqrt{2}$ Hz. What is the sum of these frequencies?
A canvas has a width of $4sqrt{3}$ inches and a length of $2sqrt{3}$ inches. What is the difference between the width and length?
If a guitarist plays a note with a frequency of $2sqrt{5}$ Hz and then plays another note with a frequency of $3sqrt{5}$ Hz, what is the product of these frequencies?
A DJ wants to create a rhythm pattern with a duration of $6sqrt{2}$ seconds and another pattern with a duration of $3sqrt{2}$ seconds. What is the ratio of these durations?
A painter has two sets of brushes with lengths $9sqrt{3}$ inches and $3sqrt{3}$ inches. What is the ratio of the lengths of these brushes?
A drummer plays a rhythm with a frequency of $4sqrt{5}$ Hz. If the drummer wants to play a harmony with a frequency that is $ rac{3}{2}$ times the original frequency, what is the new frequency?
A music teacher has two students who play guitar with frequencies $2sqrt{2}$ Hz and $3sqrt{2}$ Hz. What is the product of these frequencies?
An artist creates a painting with dimensions $6sqrt{3}$ inches by $2sqrt{3}$ inches. What is the ratio of the width to the length?

A composer writes a piece of music with a tempo of $3sqrt{2}$ beats per minute. If the composer wants to increase the tempo by a factor of $ rac{4}{3}$, what is the new tempo? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

A sculptor creates a sculpture with a height of $9sqrt{5}$ inches and a base of $3sqrt{5}$ inches. If the sculptor wants to create a smaller version with a height that is $ rac{2}{3}$ times the original height, what is the new base dimension? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Make sure to check the units of the answers.
Encourage students to show their work and explain their reasoning.
Remind students to use the properties of radicals to simplify their answers.
